### 1. Task outcome (short version):
The typical American names for steam locomotives with a 2-8-4 wheel configuration are "Berkshire" and "Kanawha".

### 2. Task outcome (extremely detailed version):
The 2-8-4 wheel configuration was first introduced by the Lima Locomotive Works, which designed the locomotives to improve upon existing designs. The name "Berkshire" was adopted by the Boston & Albany Railroad following successful tests of the new locomotives in the Berkshire Hills. Other railroads, like the Chesapeake and Ohio, referred to the same type as "Kanawha." Both "Berkshire" and "Kanawha" denote heavy-duty mainline steam locomotives and were used by various railroads across the United States, contributing to their iconic status in American railroad history.

### 3. Additional context (if relevant):
The document serves as an informational overview of the 2-8-4 "Berkshire" locomotives in the USA. It discusses their historical development, the innovations introduced by Lima Locomotive Works, and various railroads that utilized these locomotives, including their performance during tests.
